acceptance_criteria: A docker-compose.yml exists at the repo root that brings up the Django app (and any required services). README documents 'docker compose up' as the launch command. The app is reachable at localhost after compose up.
close_reason: Superseded by FastAPI migration (c42 epic). Django deployment is no longer needed — iy7 covers Docker Compose for FastAPI instead.
description: The M6 extra credit milestone explicitly requires the Django application to be deployed via Docker Compose. The whispers/ Django app is functionally complete but there is no Dockerfile or docker-compose.yml anywhere in the project. Without these, the M6 requirement is only partially satisfied (7/10 in current grading).
title: Add Docker Compose for M6 Django deployment

description:
Evaluate https://github.com/livekit/livekit for potential use in this project.

Context: The pipeline currently processes video offline (download → transcribe → translate → TTS → stitch). LiveKit is an open-source real-time WebRTC infrastructure framework.

Questions to answer:
1. Can LiveKit replace or augment the current audio track injection (moviepy/ffmpeg stitch step)?
2. Is LiveKit useful for live dubbing use cases (streaming TTS audio synchronized with live video)?
3. What is the latency profile vs the current batch pipeline?
4. Does LiveKit's Agents SDK (Python) integrate with the existing PydanticAI agent pattern?
5. What hosting/infrastructure requirements does LiveKit add vs the current Docker Compose setup?

Deliverables:
- Short technical assessment (1-2 pages)
- Go/no-go recommendation for integrating LiveKit into a future pipeline milestone
title: eval: LiveKit for real-time audio/video in foreign-whispers pipeline

close_reason: Implemented VTT caption generation and audio-only remux. GET /api/captions/{video_id} serves WebVTT. Stitch uses ffmpeg remux (instant). Frontend uses <track> element.
description:
After translation, generate SRT or VTT subtitle files from the translated segments JSON. These sidecar caption files can be served alongside the video and loaded via the HTML5 <track> element, avoiding the need to burn subtitles into the video (which is slow and prevents toggling).

**Current state:** Subtitles are burned into the video via moviepy TextClip compositing during the render stage. This is slow (~10 min CPU), inflexible (can't toggle off), and causes the corrupt-video issues we've seen.

**Proposed:**
1. After translate stage, generate .vtt file from translated segments JSON
2. Serve the .vtt via a new API endpoint GET /api/captions/{video_id}
3. Frontend video player uses <track src=... kind=subtitles> for overlay
4. Render stage becomes optional (just audio replacement, no subtitle burn-in)
title: Generate translated captions as SRT/VTT sidecar files

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tts/{video_id} produces a WAV where each segment's duration matches the source timestamp window. The TTS model is not reloaded between calls. The endpoint runs in a thread pool to avoid blocking.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/tts/{video_id} in routers/tts.py. Uses TTS model from app.state (loaded once at startup). Runs in thread pool to avoid blocking. Caches WAV output. Added tts_engine parameter to text_file_to_speech() so FastAPI can inject its model. 4 tests.
description: Expose POST /api/tts accepting video_id and target_language. Use tts_es.py as the service layer but with the timestamp synchronisation fix from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-ccu applied: per-segment audio generation with pyrubberband time-stretching and silence padding. Return the path to the generated WAV. TTS model should be loaded at startup, not per-request.
title: Port TTS + audio-sync endpoint to FastAPI

close_reason: Closed

acceptance_criteria: The credentials file requirement is documented in the README with instructions for obtaining it. A .gitignore entry exists for the file. The script degrades gracefully (falls back to youtube-transcript-api) when the credentials file is not present.
close_reason: Closed
description: download_video_captions.py requires a credentials_desktop_app.json file for Google OAuth authentication against the YouTube Data API. This file is not included in the repository (correctly, as it contains secrets), but there is no .env.example, no documentation of what the file must contain, and no fallback to the simpler youtube-transcript-api flow when the credentials file is absent. Anyone attempting to run the script gets an immediate FileNotFoundError.
title: OAuth credentials file missing from submission

close_reason: Studio layout complete: three-column resizable layout with media library, video canvas, and control panel. All 10 plan tasks done.
description:
## Overview
Revamp the frontend into a Three-Column Studio Layout for the dubbing/alignment pipeline. Maintain a functioning app at all times throughout the revamp.

## Layout Architecture

### Left Sidebar — Asset Library & Project Info
- Shadcn ScrollArea + Card components
- Video file thumbnails with Dropdown Menu for batch actions
- Project info panel

### Center Workspace — Video Preview Canvas
- Large canvas area using Shadcn AspectRatio
- Before/After comparison toggle with draggable vertical slider divider
  - Left: Original audio
  - Right: Dubbed/aligned audio
- Tabs for Single View / Split View / Side-by-Side comparison modes

### Right Panel — Controls & Parameters
- Dense configuration panel using Tabs + Accordion + Sliders
- **Dubbing Method** (top): Dropdown or radio group
  - 'Baseline (no alignment)' → FW_ALIGNMENT=off
  - 'Aligned (syllable-based stretch)' → FW_ALIGNMENT=on  ← NEW from original issue
- **Enhancement Stack** (Accordion groups):
  - Translation: language pair selector
  - TTS Engine: model selector
  - Alignment: stretch clamp sliders (SPEED_MIN/SPEED_MAX visual)
  - Audio: denoising slider, gain control

### Bottom Drawer — Process Queue
- Shadcn Resizable panel group or Sheet (collapsible)
- Progress bar per clip in the queue
- Status: queued / processing / complete / failed

## Shadcn Components to Use
- Command (Cmd+K) for quick model/video search
- Tooltip on every slider explaining the parameter
- Dialog (Modal) for export settings: format, bitrate, destination
- Switch for toggling alignment on/off
- Popover for color/preset options

## Visual Aesthetic — Studio Dark Theme
- Background: zinc-950 or slate-950
- Borders: zinc-800
- Accent: indigo-500 or emerald-500 for primary action buttons

## Component File Structure (Next.js)
- EditorCanvas.tsx — main video viewport with Before/After slider
- ModelSettings.tsx — accordion-based right sidebar
- MediaLibrary.tsx — left sidebar for uploaded files
- ProcessQueue.tsx — bottom drawer with progress bars
- ExportDialog.tsx — Shadcn Dialog for export settings

## API Integration
- Dubbing method selection maps to FW_ALIGNMENT env var in TTS service call
- Frontend sends method param to API; API sets FW_ALIGNMENT accordingly
- Selection persists across page interactions for the session

## Constraints
- Incremental rollout: each component added without breaking existing pipeline
- Existing video processing flow (download → transcribe → translate → TTS → stitch) must remain intact
- Use existing Shadcn setup; add new components as needed
title: feat(frontend): dubbing method selector (baseline vs aligned)

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/transcribe/{video_id} triggers transcription without blocking the event loop. The response (or polled status) contains segments with start/end/text. The Whisper model is not reloaded between calls.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/transcribe/{video_id} endpoint in routers/transcribe.py. Uses Whisper model from app.state (loaded at startup, not per-request). Persists transcription to JSON. Skips re-transcription for cached results. Returns 404 for missing videos. 4 tests in test_transcribe_router.py.
description: Expose POST /api/transcribe accepting a video_id. Run Whisper transcription via transcribe.py service layer using the model loaded at startup (not re-loaded per request). Return structured segments JSON. Since transcription is long-running, the endpoint should either run in a thread pool (run_in_executor) or return a job_id immediately and expose GET /api/transcribe/{job_id}/status for polling.
title: Port transcription endpoint to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: translate_all_files() (or its caller in app.py) only translates the specific video being processed. Previously translated files are not reprocessed.
close_reason: Closed
description: translate_all_files() in translate_en_to_es.py iterates over every JSON file in the transcriptions_en/ directory and overwrites the corresponding file in transcriptions_es/. When the Streamlit app processes a new video, this function retranslates all previously processed videos unnecessarily. For 10 videos this adds significant latency and wastes argostranslate inference. The function should accept a specific file path or video ID to scope its work.
title: translate_all_files() reprocesses entire directory on every run

acceptance_criteria: All file paths are derived from a single configurable base directory (e.g., a constant at the top of app.py or an env variable). No path literals are duplicated across files.
close_reason: Removed all hardcoded path literals from translated_output.py. stitch_audio() now takes explicit path params. __main__ block uses sys.argv. 2 tests in test_path_portability.py.
description: Multiple files assume a fixed directory layout relative to the script working directory: stitch_video_with_timestamps() in translated_output.py constructs paths like './ui/raw_video/', './ui/translated_audio/', etc. The video_cached() check in the Django app (whispers/vid2vid/views.py) uses a hardcoded BASE_PATH. app.py also uses inline string literals for all intermediate directories. Moving any file or running from a different working directory causes silent failures or FileNotFoundErrors.
title: Hardcoded file paths break portability

The endpoint's purpose is evaluation: run VAD, compute global alignment metrics, produce a report. Rename to /api/eval. Backend-only change — frontend doesn't reference this endpoint. Files: api/src/routers/align.py → eval.py, api/src/schemas/align.py → eval.py, update import in main.py.
title: Rename /api/align endpoint to /api/eval

description:
Restructure the foreign-whispers repo from its current hybrid state (legacy root-level utility modules + partially-built FastAPI routers) into a clean, production-grade FastAPI application following the layered architecture pattern demonstrated by Kokoro-FastAPI.

**Reference:** https://github.com/remsky/Kokoro-FastAPI

**Target architecture:**
```
foreign-whispers/
├── api/
│   └── src/
│       ├── main.py              # FastAPI app + lifespan
│       ├── core/
│       │   ├── config.py        # Pydantic BaseSettings
│       │   └── dependencies.py  # Shared DI providers
│       ├── routers/
│       │   ├── pipeline.py      # Full pipeline orchestration
│       │   ├── download.py
│       │   ├── transcribe.py
│       │   ├── translate.py
│       │   ├── tts.py
│       │   └── stitch.py
│       ├── services/            # Business logic (no HTTP concerns)
│       │   ├── download_service.py
│       │   ├── transcription_service.py
│       │   ├── translation_service.py
│       │   ├── tts_service.py
│       │   ├── stitch_service.py
│       │   └── storage_service.py  # S3 abstraction
│       ├── inference/           # ML model backends
│       │   ├── whisper_backend.py
│       │   └── tts_backend.py
│       └── schemas/             # Pydantic request/response models
│           ├── download.py
│           ├── transcription.py
│           ├── translation.py
│           ├── tts.py
│           └── pipeline.py
├── docker/
│   ├── cpu/
│   └── gpu/
├── tests/
├── speakers/
└── pyproject.toml
```

**External integrations (auraison platform):**
- **Model serving:** Whisper + TTS served via vLLM / Ray Serve on KubeRay (torch.dev.gpu environment), not loaded in-process
- **Object storage:** S3 via s3.aegean.ai (MinIO on TrueNAS, Cloudflare tunnel) — all intermediate/output artifacts stored in S3 instead of local ./ui/ filesystem
- **Database:** PostgreSQL for job tracking, video metadata, and processing state (replaces filesystem-based caching)
- **Control plane:** Submit GPU-heavy work as Ray Jobs via auraison control plane API (POST /api/v1/jobs)

**Key principles:**
1. Routers are thin — validate input, call service, return response
2. Services contain all business logic, are HTTP-agnostic and testable
3. Inference layer wraps ML model calls (local or remote via vLLM/Ray Serve)
4. Schemas define all request/response contracts
5. Configuration via Pydantic BaseSettings + .env
6. Dependency injection via FastAPI Depends()
7. No legacy code at repo root — everything under api/src/
title: FastAPI restructure: Kokoro-style layered architecture with auraison integration

Update .gitignore and README.md references. No blockers — do this first.

Routers tested with TestClient + overridden Depends(). Inference tested with mock model responses. Add conftest.py with shared fixtures (test DB, mock S3, mock models). Migrate existing test_*.py content to new structure. Fix failing TTS audio sync tests (issue ccu).

description: Add api/src/services/job_service.py that submits heavy GPU work (transcription, TTS) to auraison control plane via POST /api/v1/jobs. Foreign-whispers acts as a user-plane app: submits job with video_id + parameters, receives callback webhook when complete, retrieves artifacts from S3. This offloads GPU work to KubeRay torch.dev.gpu workers. Optional — only used when settings.use_remote_inference = true.

Remove all references to legacy Streamlit app, root-level utility modules, and Django.

Each service class is HTTP-agnostic, injectable, and testable. Services: DownloadService, TranscriptionService, TranslationService, TTSService, StitchService. Delete the original root-level files after migration. The Streamlit app.py is also removed (replaced by FastAPI).

close_reason: Centralized Pydantic schemas in api/src/schemas/, routers updated. 76 tests pass.

Use Pydantic v2 model_validator where needed. These replace the implicit dict/JSON structures currently passed between modules.

close_reason: Scaffolded api/src/ package with main.py, core/config.py, core/dependencies.py, routers, schemas, services. All 59 tests pass.
description: Create the api/src/ package layout: main.py (FastAPI app with lifespan), core/config.py (Pydantic BaseSettings with S3, PostgreSQL, vLLM endpoints, CORS, model config), core/dependencies.py (Depends providers for services and DB session). Move existing main.py and core/ content. Settings should read from .env with sensible defaults for local dev. Reference: Kokoro-FastAPI core/config.py pattern.
title: Scaffold api/src/ package structure with main.py and core/config.py

description: Refactor existing routers (download, transcribe, translate, tts, stitch) to be thin HTTP handlers: validate input via schemas, inject service via Depends(), call service method, return response. Remove all business logic from routers. Add a new pipeline router that orchestrates the full video translation flow. All routers move under api/src/routers/.
title: Refactor routers to thin wrappers over services

close_reason: Inference layer with local/remote backends. 17 tests pass.
description: Create api/src/inference/ with WhisperBackend and TTSBackend classes. Each backend has a local mode (load model in-process, current behavior) and a remote mode (call vLLM/Ray Serve endpoint on auraison KubeRay cluster). Backend selection via config (settings.whisper_backend = "local" | "remote"). Remote mode sends gRPC/HTTP requests to model serving endpoints. This decouples the app from heavy GPU dependencies.
title: Create inference layer for Whisper and TTS backends

close_reason: Storage service with local/S3 backends. 14 tests pass.
description: Create api/src/services/storage_service.py wrapping S3 operations via boto3/aioboto3. All pipeline artifacts (raw videos, transcriptions, translations, audio, final video) stored in s3.aegean.ai buckets instead of local ./ui/ directories. Bucket layout: s3://foreign-whispers/{video_id}/raw.mp4, transcription_en.json, transcription_es.json, audio_es.wav, output.mp4. StorageService injected into other services via Depends(). Local filesystem fallback for dev without S3.
title: Add S3 storage service replacing local filesystem cache

close_reason: SQLAlchemy async models, engine factory, get_db dependency. Tests skip when sqlalchemy absent.
description: Replace filesystem-based caching (video_cached() in app.py) with PostgreSQL. Tables: videos (id, url, title, status, language, s3_prefix, created_at), pipeline_jobs (id, video_id, stage, status, started_at, completed_at, error). Use SQLAlchemy async with asyncpg. Connection via settings.database_url. Alembic for migrations. DB session injected via Depends(get_db). This enables proper job tracking and multi-worker processing.
title: Add PostgreSQL for job tracking and video metadata

description: Replace single Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml with Kokoro-style docker/ directory: docker/cpu/ and docker/gpu/ each with Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml. CPU variant runs with remote model inference (no torch/CUDA). GPU variant includes torch+CUDA for local inference. Both compose files define: api (FastAPI), postgres, minio (dev only). Production compose connects to s3.aegean.ai and external postgres. Remove Streamlit service from compose.
title: Docker multi-stage build with cpu/gpu variants

description: Instrument the existing pipeline stages (download, transcribe, translate, TTS, stitch) with Logfire spans and structured fields per the architecture doc at docs/pydanticai-logfire-architecture.md. Currently only instrument_fastapi is wired up — need per-stage spans with domain metrics (segment_index, source_duration_ms, stretch_factor, etc).
title: Add Logfire tracing spans to pipeline stages

close_reason: Implemented POST /api/download endpoint in routers/download.py. Validates YouTube URLs (422 for invalid), skips re-download for cached files (issue fo6 guard), reads caption segments from line-delimited JSON. 3 tests in test_download_router.py.

Expose POST /api/download accepting a YouTube URL and returning video_id plus the downloaded transcript as JSON. Use the existing download_video.py and download_video_captions.py modules as the service layer — do not duplicate their logic. Add the re-download guard from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-fo6 as part of this work.

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/translate/{video_id} translates only that video's transcript. Previously translated videos are not reprocessed. Response contains translated segments with timestamps preserved.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/translate/{video_id} in routers/translate.py. Translates a single video transcript (fixes 5ss scope bug — no directory sweep). Caches results, returns 404 for missing sources. 4 tests.
description: Expose POST /api/translate accepting video_id and target_language. Use translate_en_to_es.py as the service layer. Fix the scope bug from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-5ss (translate only the specified video, not the entire directory) as part of this work. Return translated segments JSON.
title: Port translation endpoint to FastAPI

close_reason: Epic complete. All 7 subtasks closed: skeleton (r7s), download (by5), transcribe (58f), translate (c0m), TTS (381), stitch (fzm), Docker Compose (iy7). FastAPI API has 5 endpoint routers, 42 total tests passing.
description: The current M6 extra credit implementation uses Django (whispers/ app) to serve the translation pipeline. Django is heavyweight for this use case: it brings ORM, admin, sessions, and auth middleware that are unused. FastAPI is a better fit — async-native (important since Whisper, TTS, and video processing are all long-running), automatic OpenAPI docs, and lighter to containerise for the Docker Compose requirement. This epic covers replacing the whispers/ Django app with a FastAPI backend while keeping app.py (Streamlit) as the frontend.
title: Migrate M6 backend from Django to FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: Each TTS segment is stretched/compressed to match the duration of its source timestamp window. Silent padding is inserted for gaps between segments. The final WAV is assembled by concatenating in timestamp order. Dubbed video is audibly in sync with lip movement.
close_reason: Implemented audio-video sync in tts_es.py: per-segment TTS generation, pyrubberband time-stretching, silence gap padding. All 8 tests pass. Also added XTTSClient for Docker API with local Coqui fallback.
description: tts_es.py generates a single concatenated WAV file by joining all translated segments end-to-end at the TTS model's natural speaking rate. There is no per-segment time-stretching or silence padding to align speech to original speaker timestamps. When the dubbed audio is laid over the video, timing drifts immediately and compounds across the full duration. The correct approach is to generate each segment as a separate audio file, use pyrubberband.time_stretch() to compress or expand it to fit its target timestamp window, insert AudioSegment.silent() for gaps between segments, then concatenate in order to produce the final WAV.
title: TTS output has no audio-video timestamp synchronisation

acceptance_criteria: Before downloading, the script checks whether the target MP4 and caption JSON files already exist. Existing files are skipped with a log message.
close_reason: Closed
description: download_video() in download_video.py does not check whether a video file already exists before calling pytube to download it. Running the script twice will re-download all 10 videos, overwriting existing files. This is slow and unnecessary. The youtube-transcript-api caption download has the same issue.
title: download_video.py re-downloads videos on every run

description: Expose POST /api/stitch accepting video_id to produce the final dubbed video via translated_output.py. Expose GET /api/video/{video_id} to stream the output MP4 using FileResponse. Fix the ImageMagick dependency issue from beads issue foreign-whispers-projects-ttl and the hardcoded path issue from foreign-whispers-projects-6e1 as part of this work.

description: M5a of the spec explicitly requires the Streamlit UI to be deployed on Hugging Face Spaces (Streamlit Spaces). app.py is a complete Streamlit implementation but it only runs locally. No HF Space exists. This is a graded milestone requirement worth 15 of the 20 M5a points.

description: 13-task TDD implementation: 6 library modules, 3 service extensions, 2 API routes, wheel build. See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-18-foreign-whispers-library.md
title: Implement foreign_whispers library

close_reason: Kokoro does not support Spanish. Replacing with Coqui XTTS-v2.
description: Replace Coqui TTS (tts_models/es/mai/tacotron2-DDC) with Kokoro-FastAPI (github.com/remsky/Kokoro-FastAPI) as the TTS engine. Kokoro-FastAPI provides a Dockerized OpenAI-compatible speech API with GPU acceleration (CUDA 12.8, 35-100x realtime). CAVEAT: Kokoro currently supports English, Japanese, Chinese only — Spanish support is NOT yet available. This issue is blocked until Kokoro adds Spanish, or we use it for English TTS and keep a separate Spanish engine. Steps: (1) Add Kokoro-FastAPI as a Docker service in compose, (2) Replace tts_es.py TTS calls with OpenAI-compatible API calls to Kokoro endpoint, (3) Keep audio sync logic (_synced_segment_audio) intact, (4) Update pyproject.toml — remove Coqui TTS dep, add openai client.
notes: Closed: Kokoro does not support Spanish. Using Coqui XTTS-v2 instead.
title: Migrate TTS engine from Coqui TTS to Kokoro-FastAPI

acceptance_criteria: uvicorn main:app --reload starts without errors. GET /healthz returns 200. OpenAPI docs are available at /docs. Whisper and TTS models are loaded once in the lifespan context.
close_reason: Closed
description: Create the FastAPI entry point (main.py) with app instantiation, CORS middleware, and lifespan hooks for loading the Whisper model and TTS model once at startup (rather than per-request as the current Django views do). Establish the directory layout: routers/, services/, schemas/ — mirroring the existing Django apps but without ORM machinery. Add fastapi, uvicorn[standard], and python-multipart to requirements.txt.
title: Define FastAPI app skeleton and project layout

description:
Two issues in the dubbed video output:

**Issue 1: Subtitle positioning (translated_output.py)**
- TextClips are created with .set_pos(('center', 'bottom')) per clip
- But then concatenate_videoclips() is called which destroys individual positions
- Result: subtitles appear at top-left corner instead of centered bottom
- Root cause: line 113 concatenates instead of compositing

**Issue 2: TTS temporal misalignment (tts_es.py)**
- Each Spanish TTS segment is time-stretched via pyrubberband to match the English segment duration
- Spanish text is typically 15-30% longer than English, so TTS audio is often longer than the English window
- Extreme speed factors cause unnatural speech (too fast when Spanish is much longer, too slow for short segments)
- The speed_factor clamp [0.1, 10] is too wide — perceptually acceptable range is ~[0.8, 1.3]
- No prosody-aware stretching — rubber band pitch-shifts but doesnt preserve naturalness

**Design doc needed in docs/ covering solutions from the literature.**
title: Fix subtitle positioning and TTS temporal alignment in dubbed video

acceptance_criteria: requirements.txt or a setup script installs or validates ImageMagick. README documents the system dependency with the install command for common platforms. moviepy IMAGEMAGICK_BINARY is set correctly at startup.
close_reason: Auto-detect ImageMagick binary via PATH/env var instead of hardcoded macOS path. Added imagemagick to Dockerfile apt-get. 3 tests in test_imagemagick.py.
description: stitch_video_with_timestamps() in translated_output.py uses moviepy TextClip objects, which require ImageMagick to be installed system-wide. ImageMagick is not listed in requirements.txt and is not mentioned anywhere in the README or documentation. The app fails at runtime with a cryptic moviepy error when ImageMagick is absent. Additionally, moviepy must be configured with the correct ImageMagick binary path via IMAGEMAGICK_BINARY.
title: Add ImageMagick as explicit dependency and document install

close_reason: Closed
description: Decide on the right license for the foreign_whispers Python library and the main repo. Considerations: instructor solution (source hidden from students), wheel-distributed, educational use, open-source components used (argostranslate/OpenNMT, Coqui TTS, Whisper). Options include MIT, Apache 2.0, AGPL, CC-BY-NC, or a custom educational license. May also need to consider dual licensing (open for instructors, restricted for commercial use).
title: Brainstorm: appropriate license for foreign_whispers
